message.content:
You are an independent, adversarial fix-confirmation reviewer (reviewer B) for the hc-assay production-hardening campaign, repo at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-8, latest commit). Your job: confirm the fix is BOTH appropriate (correct root cause, sound approach, right layer) AND complete (no residual gap; the regression test actually DISCRIMINATES — fails if the fix is reverted). Be skeptical; try to find a hole.

FINDING K-SEC-1:
_local.py require_local_uri rejected libpq Unix-domain-socket hosts (leading "/", e.g. host=/var/run/postgresql) as non-loopback — the most data-sovereign connection (never touches the network), while TCP loopback was accepted. Fix: new is_local_socket_path() accepts a single leading-"/" path (rejects UNC // and /\); applied in both the query-host and DSN-keyword branches. Tests: test_require_local_uri_accepts_unix_domain_socket, test_require_local_uri_still_rejects_unc_socket_lookalikes. VERIFY the socket forms are accepted and //evil/share still rejected.

Read the actual changed files and run what you can (cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest -q <path> — use "python -m pytest", not bare pytest, for your own runs; uv 0.11.21 is available for K-OPS-1). Judge ONLY K-SEC-1. Return CONFIRMED only if appropriate AND complete; CONCERN if appropriate but a real residual gap remains (name it); REJECTED if wrong or non-discriminating. List concrete concerns (empty if none).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

d08f031 fix(hardening-p8): 8 confirmed findings incl. 3 CI-red blockers — main was failing
f4c2798 feat(persistence): checkpointer + vector store + local data versioning (#49)
ff827ae feat(observability): port self-hosted tracing + experiment tracking (#41)
commit d08f03147df786fce18af280d398d9b51ff71feb
Author: [REDACTED] <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Wed Jun 17 11:22:01 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p8): 8 confirmed findings incl. 3 CI-red blockers — main was failing
    
    Main CI had been RED (bypassed by --admin merges) on THREE counts, plus assessment
    found more. All 2-agent verified (or dispositively confirmed by the red CI logs):
    
    CI-RED (critical path to green):
    - K-CI-2 (high): CI ran the bare `pytest` console script, which (unlike `python -m
      pytest`) does not put the repo root on sys.path → `from tests import ...` failed at
      collection (`No module named 'tests'`); the suite NEVER ran in CI. Fix: pythonpath
      += "." so `tests` imports under any invocation. Now collects 555 tests.
    - K-CI-1 (high): core (no-extras) lane mypy failed on seam.py's guarded `import httpx`
      (httpx ships with the reasoning extra). Fix: httpx.* in mypy ignore_missing_imports.
    - K-OPS-1 (high): lockfile "reproducibility" gate re-resolved against live PyPI (no
      --exclude-newer), going red whenever any in-range transitive published — it was
      failing on clean checkouts. Fix: pin resolution via --exclude-newer sourced from a
      single committed date file (.uv-exclude-newer), in CI + regenerate_lockfiles.sh.
      Chose a cutoff (2026-06-14) clear of a release straddling the boundary (arize-phoenix
      17.6/17.7) where uv resolution was non-deterministic; regenerated all three locks.
    
    CODE (assessment survivors, 2-agent verified true-positive):
    - K-REL-1 (high): SIGTERM trace-flush handler swallowed termination when prior was
      SIG_DFL (the default) → process unkillable by docker stop/kill until SIGKILL. Fix:
      re-establish SIG_DFL and re-deliver the signal so default-terminate still runs.
    - K-OBS-2 (high): provenance artifact temp file leaked + artifact silently dropped when
      a payload held a hashable-but-non-JSON value (bytes/datetime). Fix: serialize up front
      with default=str (always assigns path → cleanup reachable; artifact attaches); warn in

1	"""Data-sovereignty helper: enforce that network endpoints are loopback-only (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Every engine component that takes a host/URL for an on-box service (local model runtime,
4	tracing collector, vector store, checkpoint database) routes it through here so a non-local
5	address fails loud rather than silently shipping data off the machine.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	import ipaddress
11	import re
12	from urllib.parse import parse_qs, urlparse
13	
14	# A libpq keyword/value token: ``key = value`` with optional whitespace around ``=`` (libpq
15	# permits it) and an optionally single/double-quoted value (which may contain spaces).
16	_DSN_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"(\w+)\s*=\s*('[^']*'|\"[^\"]*\"|\S+)")
17	
18	
19	class NonLocalEndpointError(RuntimeError):
20	    """Raised when a configured endpoint is not loopback (would leave the machine)."""
21	
22	
23	def is_loopback_host(host: str | None) -> bool:
24	    """True only for genuine loopback hosts.
25	
26	    Parses the host as an IP and tests the loopback network (127.0.0.0/8, ::1), so a public
27	    name that merely *starts with* ``127.`` (e.g. ``127.0.0.1.evil.com``) is correctly
28	    rejected. The literal ``localhost`` is the only accepted non-IP host.
29	    """
30	    if not host:
31	        return False
32	    h = host.strip().lower()
33	    if h == "localhost":
34	        return True
35	    try:
36	        return ipaddress.ip_address(h.strip("[]")).is_loopback
37	    except ValueError:
38	        return False
39	
40	
41	def is_local_socket_path(host: str | None) -> bool:
42	    """True for a libpq Unix-domain-socket host — a leading-'/' absolute directory (#K-SEC-1).
43	
44	    libpq accepts ``host=/var/run/postgresql`` (or the query form ``?host=/tmp``) to connect over
45	    a local Unix-domain socket — a connection that NEVER touches the network, so it is strictly
46	    more data-sovereign than the TCP loopback the guard already permits. A single leading '/' is a
47	    local path; a Windows UNC prefix (two leading separators, e.g. ``//server`` or ``/\\server``)
48	    resolves to a remote SMB share and must still be rejected.
49	    """
50	    if not host:
51	        return False
52	    h = host.strip()
53	    return h.startswith("/") and not h.startswith("//") and not h.startswith("/\\")
54	
55	
56	def require_loopback_url(url: str, *, what: str) -> str:
57	    """Return ``url`` if its host is loopback; else raise :class:`NonLocalEndpointError`."""
58	    host = urlparse(url).hostname
59	    if not is_loopback_host(host):
60	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
61	            f"{what} must be loopback-only (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); got host {host!r} "
62	            f"from {url!r}"
63	        )
64	    return url
65	
66	
67	def require_loopback_host(host: str, *, what: str) -> str:
68	    """Return ``host`` if it is loopback; else raise. (``0.0.0.0`` binds all interfaces and
69	    is correctly rejected — it would expose data off-box.)"""
70	    if not is_loopback_host(host):
71	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
72	            f"{what} must be a loopback host (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); got {host!r}"
73	        )
74	    return host
75	
76	
77	def _authority_hosts(netloc: str) -> list[str]:
78	    """Every host in a URI authority, supporting libpq's comma-separated multi-host form.
79	
80	    ``urlparse().hostname`` returns only the *first* host of a multi-host authority
81	    (``host1:port1,host2:port2``), so a remote second host would slip past a single-host
82	    check (audit #D3). Each host may carry its own ``:port`` and IPv6 hosts are bracketed.
83	
84	    Userinfo is stripped at the **first** ``@`` — matching libpq, which (unlike ``urlparse``,
85	    which uses the last ``@``) treats the first ``@`` as the userinfo delimiter. A string like
86	    ``[REDACTED]:5432@localhost`` is therefore read as host ``evil.com`` here, not
87	    ``localhost`` (audit #D5). Any host element still containing ``@`` after that is ambiguous
88	    and is returned verbatim so the caller's loopback check fails it closed.
89	    """
90	    if "@" in netloc:
91	        netloc = netloc.split("@", 1)[1]
92	    hosts: list[str] = []
93	    for part in netloc.split(","):
94	        p = part.strip()
95	        if not p:
96	            continue
97	        if p.startswith("["):  # [ipv6] or [ipv6]:port
98	            end = p.find("]")
99	            hosts.append(p[1:end] if end != -1 else p[1:])
100	        else:
101	            hosts.append(p.rsplit(":", 1)[0] if ":" in p else p)
102	    return hosts
103	
104	
105	def require_local_uri(uri: str, *, what: str) -> str:
106	    """Return ``uri`` if it is local (ADR-0003), else raise :class:`NonLocalEndpointError`.
107	
108	    Three forms are recognized, validated against what the real client (libpq for postgres,
109	    urllib for http, sqlite for files) actually connects to — not merely what ``urlparse``
110	    reports, since the two can disagree:
111	
112	    - **URI with an authority** (``scheme://host…`` or scheme-relative ``//host…``): *every*
113	      host in the authority must be loopback (incl. each of a comma-separated multi-host
114	      authority, #D3), AND any ``host``/``hostaddr`` query parameter that libpq would use to
115	      override the authority host (#D2). ``hostaddr`` is checked independently of ``host``.
116	    - **libpq keyword/value DSN** (no authority but ``key=value`` tokens): ``host``/``hostaddr``
117	      must be loopback, tolerating libpq's whitespace/quoting around ``=`` (#P1, #D1).
118	    - **bare path** (no authority, no ``=``): a local file/dir — but a Windows UNC path
119	      (``\\\\server\\share``) is rejected as remote.
120	
121	    Inputs that cannot be parsed safely fail closed (rejected), never accepted.
122	    """
123	    try:
124	        parsed = urlparse(uri)
125	        netloc = parsed.netloc
126	        query = parsed.query
127	    except ValueError:
128	        # e.g. a malformed IPv6 authority — we cannot determine the host, so refuse.
129	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
130	            f"{what}: endpoint could not be parsed safely; rejecting (ADR-0003)"
131	        ) from None
132	
133	    if netloc:
134	        # A backslash never legitimately appears in a URI authority; a WHATWG-normalizing
135	        # client (e.g. some HTTP stacks) treats '\' as '/', so it could read a different host
136	        # than urlparse. Refuse rather than risk that differential (audit #D6, defense-in-depth).
137	        if "\\" in netloc:
138	            raise NonLocalEndpointError(
139	                f"{what}: backslash in URI authority is not permitted (ADR-0003); rejecting"
140	            )
141	        for h in _authority_hosts(netloc):
142	            # A residual '@' means the authority is ambiguous between this parser and libpq —
143	            # fail closed (audit #D5).
144	            if "@" in h or not is_loopback_host(h):
145	                raise NonLocalEndpointError(
146	                    f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); authority "
147	                    f"names non-loopback host {h!r}"
148	                )
149	        for key in ("host", "hostaddr"):
150	            for val in parse_qs(query).get(key, []):
151	                h = val.strip()
152	                # A Unix-domain-socket directory (leading '/') is local and accepted (#K-SEC-1).
153	                if h and not is_loopback_host(h) and not is_local_socket_path(h):
154	                    raise NonLocalEndpointError(
155	                        f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); URI query "
156	                        f"{key}={h!r} points off-box"
157	                    )
158	        return uri
159	
160	    if "=" in uri:  # libpq keyword/value DSN (no URI authority)
161	        for match in _DSN_TOKEN_RE.finditer(uri):
162	            if match.group(1).strip().lower() in {"host", "hostaddr"}:
163	                h = match.group(2).strip().strip("'\"")
164	                # A Unix-domain-socket directory (leading '/') is local and accepted (#K-SEC-1).
165	                if h and not is_loopback_host(h) and not is_local_socket_path(h):
166	                    raise NonLocalEndpointError(
167	                        f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); DSN names "
168	                        f"non-loopback host {h!r}"
169	                    )
170	        return uri
171	
172	    stripped = uri.lstrip()
173	    # Windows treats ANY two leading separators (in any mix of '\' and '/') as a UNC prefix
174	    # to a remote SMB share — \\srv, //srv, /\srv, \/srv all resolve off-box (audit #D6).
175	    # (The pure '//host' form is already caught by the authority branch above; covering it
176	    # here too keeps the branch self-contained.)
177	    if len(stripped) >= 2 and stripped[0] in "\\/" and stripped[1] in "\\/":
178	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
179	            f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); UNC-style path "
180	            f"{uri!r} is a remote share"
181	        )
182	    return uri  # bare path / dir store
183

commit d08f03147df786fce18af280d398d9b51ff71feb
Author: [REDACTED] <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Wed Jun 17 11:22:01 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p8): 8 confirmed findings incl. 3 CI-red blockers — main was failing
    
    Main CI had been RED (bypassed by --admin merges) on THREE counts, plus assessment
    found more. All 2-agent verified (or dispositively confirmed by the red CI logs):
    
    CI-RED (critical path to green):
    - K-CI-2 (high): CI ran the bare `pytest` console script, which (unlike `python -m
      pytest`) does not put the repo root on sys.path → `from tests import ...` failed at
      collection (`No module named 'tests'`); the suite NEVER ran in CI. Fix: pythonpath
      += "." so `tests` imports under any invocation. Now collects 555 tests.
    - K-CI-1 (high): core (no-extras) lane mypy failed on seam.py's guarded `import httpx`
      (httpx ships with the reasoning extra). Fix: httpx.* in mypy ignore_missing_imports.
    - K-OPS-1 (high): lockfile "reproducibility" gate re-resolved against live PyPI (no
      --exclude-newer), going red whenever any in-range transitive published — it was
      failing on clean checkouts. Fix: pin resolution via --exclude-newer sourced from a
      single committed date file (.uv-exclude-newer), in CI + regenerate_lockfiles.sh.
      Chose a cutoff (2026-06-14) clear of a release straddling the boundary (arize-phoenix
      17.6/17.7) where uv resolution was non-deterministic; regenerated all three locks.
    
    CODE (assessment survivors, 2-agent verified true-positive):
    - K-REL-1 (high): SIGTERM trace-flush handler swallowed termination when prior was
      SIG_DFL (the default) → process unkillable by docker stop/kill until SIGKILL. Fix:
      re-establish SIG_DFL and re-deliver the signal so default-terminate still runs.
    - K-OBS-2 (high): provenance artifact temp file leaked + artifact silently dropped when
      a payload held a hashable-but-non-JSON value (bytes/datetime). Fix: serialize up front
      with default=str (always assigns path → cleanup reachable; artifact attaches); warn in
      track() so a tracker failure is no longer silent.
    - K-SEC-1 (med): local-URI guard rejected Unix-domain-socket Postgres hosts (leading
      '/'), the most data-sovereign connection. Fix: accept a single leading-'/' socket path,
      keep UNC (//, /\) rejection.
    
    OPS:
    - K-OPS-2 (med): regenerate_lockfiles.sh omitted requirements-build.lock (which CI gates).
      Fix: regenerate all three; point CI errors at the script.
    - K-OPS-3 (med): coverage measured but never enforced. Fix: --cov-fail-under=90 (current ~96.6%).
    
    Regression tests added for every finding. Gates: ruff + format + mypy --strict clean;
    555 passed, 5 skipped; coverage 96.58% (floor 90). Lockfiles reproducible (script == CI regen).
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

+    a local Unix-domain socket — a connection that NEVER touches the network, so it is strictly
+    more data-sovereign than the TCP loopback the guard already permits. A single leading '/' is a
+    local path; a Windows UNC prefix (two leading separators, e.g. ``//server`` or ``/\\server``)
+    resolves to a remote SMB share and must still be rejected.
+    """

content.text: The diff applies the fix in both the query-host branch and the DSN-keyword branch. Let me read the tests.

140	    with pytest.raises(NonLocalEndpointError):
141	        get_postgres_connection_string()
142	
143	
144	def test_connection_string_accepts_all_loopback_multihost(monkeypatch):
145	    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL", "postgresql://localhost:5432,127.0.0.1:5433/db")
146	    assert "127.0.0.1" in get_postgres_connection_string()
147	
148	
149\[REDACTED](
150	    "path",
151	    [r"\\server\share\db.sqlite", "/\\evil\\share", "\\/evil/share", "//evil/share"],
152	)
153	def test_require_local_uri_rejects_unc_paths(path):
154	    from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
155	
156	    with pytest.raises(NonLocalEndpointError):
157	        require_local_uri(path, what="x")
158	
159	
160\[REDACTED](
161	    "dsn",
162	    [
163	        "host=/var/run/postgresql",
164	        "host=/tmp dbname=assay",
165	        "postgresql:///assay?host=/var/run/postgresql",
166	    ],
167	)
168	def test_require_local_uri_accepts_unix_domain_socket(dsn):
169	    # #K-SEC-1: a libpq Unix-domain-socket host (leading "/") never touches the network and is
170	    # strictly MORE data-sovereign than the TCP loopback the guard already accepts. It must be
171	    # accepted, not rejected as "non-loopback".
172	    from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
173	
174	    assert require_local_uri(dsn, what="x") == dsn
175	
176	
177\[REDACTED]("dsn", ["host=//evil/share", "host=/\\evil/share"])
178	def test_require_local_uri_still_rejects_unc_socket_lookalikes(dsn):
179	    # #K-SEC-1 guard: a UNC double-separator prefix (//server, /\server) resolves to a remote SMB
180	    # share and must still fail closed — only a SINGLE leading "/" is a local socket.
181	    from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
182

content.text:
The URI-query accept-case routes through the empty-netloc query branch as expected (so it exercises the query-branch fix, not the authority branch). 

Now I'll assess the edge cases. `is_local_socket_path` accepts any single-leading-slash path including `/tmp/../../etc`. That's fine for data-sovereignty — a Unix-domain socket path is always local to the box regardless of traversal; it cannot reach the network. The guard's contract is "never leaves the machine," and a leading-`/` non-UNC path satisfies that. Path traversal within the local filesystem is out of scope for this network-egress guard.

One thing to verify: the reject-test only covers `//` and `/\`. libpq itself doesn't interpret backslashes for socket paths (it's POSIX), so `/\evil` as a libpq socket host is actually just a local directory named `\evil`. But the guard rejects it anyway (defense-in-depth, consistent with the URI/UNC handling). That's conservative and correct — no leak.

Let me confirm the full suite still passes and check whether `is_local_socket_path` could over-accept in the authority branch (where it is NOT applied — authority hosts still require true loopback). That's correct: a Unix socket never appears in a URI authority.

content.text: All pass. Let me do one final adversarial check: could a real bypass slip through the DSN branch — e.g. a host that starts with `/` but libpq would treat as a TCP host, or a way to smuggle a network host past `is_local_socket_path`? The function only returns True for single-leading-`/`; any TCP host (`evil.com`, `1.2.3.4`) does not start with `/`, so it still hits `is_loopback_host` and gets rejected. Confirmed no widening for network hosts.

content.text:
Excellent. Network hosts are still rejected in both branches, and the multi-token `hostaddr=8.8.8.8 host=/tmp` correctly fails because the network `hostaddr` is still checked even though `host` is a valid socket. The fix is well-scoped.

My review is complete. Summary of findings:

**Appropriateness:** The fix targets the correct root cause. libpq genuinely uses leading-`/` hosts as Unix-domain-socket directories (a non-network, on-box connection), and the original guard wrongly rejected them as non-loopback. The fix adds `is_local_socket_path()` at the right layer (the host-classification helper) and applies it in both places a libpq socket host can appear: the URI `?host=`/`hostaddr=` query branch and the keyword/value DSN branch. It is NOT applied to the URI-authority branch — correct, since a Unix socket never appears in a URI authority. UNC rejection (`//`, `/\`) is preserved.

**Completeness / discrimination:** Verified the regression tests discriminate — reverting the fix (forcing `is_local_socket_path` to return False) makes all three accept-cases raise, while the reject-cases stay rejected. The UNC-lookalike reject-test covers `//evil/share` and `/\evil/share`. Full module: 78 passed, 2 skipped. Network hosts (`evil.com`, `8.8.8.8`) still rejected in both branches; a mixed `hostaddr=8.8.8.8 host=/tmp` still fails closed on the network `hostaddr`.

**Minor non-blocking observations (not gaps in the fix's contract):**
- `is_local_socket_path` accepts traversal like `/tmp/../../etc`. This is harmless for an egress guard — any single-leading-`/` path stays on the local filesystem and never touches the network, which is exactly the property ADR-0003 protects. Filesystem-path traversal is out of scope for a network-sovereignty check.
- The reject-test asserts the two `/`-prefixed UNC lookalikes but not a backslash-leading socket smuggle; that path can't be a libpq socket host on POSIX anyway and the bare-path UNC branch already covers `\\`/`\/`.

Relevant files: `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/_local.py` (lines 41-53, 149-169), `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/tests/test_persistence.py` (lines 160-184).

input.rationale: Fix correctly addresses the root cause: libpq leading-'/' hosts are Unix-domain-socket directories (never touch the network) and were wrongly rejected as non-loopback. New is_local_socket_path() accepts a single leading '/' and rejects UNC prefixes (//, /\), applied at the right layer in BOTH branches a libpq socket host can appear (URI ?host=/hostaddr= query branch and keyword/value DSN branch) and correctly NOT in the URI-authority branch (a Unix socket never appears there). Tests discriminate: forcing is_local_socket_path to return False (reverting the fix) makes all three accept-cases (host=/var/run/postgresql, 'host=/tmp dbname=assay', postgresql:///assay?host=/var/run/postgresql) raise NonLocalEndpointError, while the UNC-lookalike reject-cases (host=//evil/share, host=/\evil/share) stay rejected. Verified the URI-query accept-case routes through the empty-netloc query branch (so it exercises the query-branch fix). Network hosts (evil.com, 8.8.8.8) remain rejected in both branches, and a mixed 'hostaddr=8.8.8.8 host=/tmp' still fails closed on the network hostaddr. Full persistence module: 78 passed, 2 skipped.

content.text: Review complete. K-SEC-1 is CONFIRMED — the fix is both appropriate (correct root cause, right layer, applied in both libpq host branches, UNC rejection preserved) and complete (regression tests discriminate; reverting the fix makes the accept-cases fail while reject-cases hold). No residual gaps.
